name: General Tao Chicken
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut chicken into pieces no larger than 3/4 inch square. Combine marinade ingredients in a large bowl. Add chicken pieces and set aside for 2 hours. Add 2 tablespoons cornstarch to the chicken stock, store in refrigerator.
2. In a deep pot, heat oil to 350°F. Using a basket or slotted spoon, lower several pieces of chicken into the oil. Fry until chicken becomes crisp (1 to 2 minutes) and test meat for doneness. Continue with several pieces at a time until all are cooked.
3. In a wok, on high heat, add reserved oil. Add prepared ginger, scallions, garlic, and chilies. Stir to prevent burning. Add the cooked chicken pieces, still stirring quickly.
4. Add sugar, soy sauce, vinegar, stock/cornstarch mixture, and stir until thick. Remove from heat, stir in sesame oil, and place on serving dish. Serve with steamed rice.
